name: syndirella
documentation:
# Syndirella
> 👑 Synthetically Directed Elaborations

Syndirella is a tool for generating and scoring synthetically practical elaborations of molecules designed from fragment screens. Starting from the retrosynthetic analysis of fragment merged molecules, superstructures of the original reactants are found by catalog search and filtered by selectivity issues. The elaborated final products are defined by reaction SMIRKS patterns and energy minimized in the protein with restraints to experimental data. Syndirella also suggests synthetic routes that are cost-efficient and could range from single to multiple steps, tailored to the user's needs.

https://syndirella.readthedocs.io/

[![Documentation Status](https://readthedocs.org/projects/syndirella/badge/?version=latest)](https://syndirella.readthedocs.io/en/latest/?badge=latest)
![Code Climate Maintainability](https://img.shields.io/codeclimate/maintainability/kate-fie/syndirella)

modules.name: syndirella.slipper.flatness
documentation:
slipper/flatness.py

This module was directly sourced from the PoseBusters repository, which is open source code under the BSD-3-Clause license.
The original code is found at: https://github.com/maabuu/posebusters/blob/3c467ab82bdbae5b71f80a286bb49ecc011be529/posebusters/modules/flatness.py

It is used to check flatness of aromatic rings and double bonds of ligand conformations.
!venv/bin/env python3

functions.name: syndirella.slipper.flatness.check_flatness
documentation:
Check whether substructures of molecule are flat.

Args:
    mol_pred: Molecule with exactly one conformer.
    threshold_flatness: Maximum distance from shared plane used as cutoff. Defaults to 0.1.
    flat_systems: Patterns of flat systems provided as SMARTS. Defaults to 5 and 6 membered
        aromatic rings and carbon sigma bonds.

Returns:
    PoseBusters results dictionary.

functions.name: syndirella.slipper.flatness._get_distances_to_plane
documentation: Get distances of points X to their common plane.

modules.name: syndirella.slipper.intra_geometry
documentation:
slipper/intra_geometry.py

This module was directly sourced from the PoseBusters repository, which is open source code under the BSD-3-Clause license.
The original code is found at: https://github.com/maabuu/posebusters/blob/3c467ab82bdbae5b71f80a286bb49ecc011be529/posebusters/modules/distance_geometry.py

It is used to check bond lengths, bond angles, and the internal clash of ligand conformations.
!venv/bin/env python3

functions.name: syndirella.slipper.intra_geometry.check_geometry
documentation:
Use RDKit distance geometry bounds to check the geometry of a molecule.

Args:
    mol_pred: Predicted molecule (docked ligand). Only the first conformer will be checked.
    threshold_bad_bond_length: Bond length threshold in relative percentage. 0.2 means that bonds may be up to 20%
        longer than DG bounds. Defaults to 0.2.
    threshold_clash: Threshold for how much overlap constitutes a clash. 0.2 means that the two atoms may be up to
        80% of the lower bound apart. Defaults to 0.2.
    threshold_bad_angle: Bond angle threshold in relative percentage. 0.2 means that bonds may be up to 20%
        longer than DG bounds. Defaults to 0.2.
    bound_matrix_params: Parameters passe to RDKit's GetMoleculeBoundsMatrix function.
    ignore_hydrogens: Whether to ignore hydrogens. Defaults to True.
    sanitize: Sanitize molecule before running DG module (recommended). Defaults to True.

Returns:
    PoseBusters results dictionary.

functions.name: syndirella.slipper.intra_geometry._get_angle_atom_indices
documentation: Check all combinations of bonds to generate list of molecule angles.

functions.name: syndirella.slipper._placement_data.make_fragmenstein_placements_csv

Args:

    library_output_dir: str: The path to the directory where the final products csv is located.

functions.name: syndirella.slipper._placement_data.get_bound_unbound
documentation: Get the bound and unbound energy values from the JSON file. Accounts for different formats

functions.name: syndirella.slipper._placement_data.make_success_csv_row
documentation: Make a row for the success.csv file.

modules.name: syndirella.slipper.SlipperFitter
documentation:
slipper_fitter/CobblersWorkshop.py

This module contains the SlipperFitter class.
!venv/bin/env python3

classes.name: syndirella.slipper.SlipperFitter.SlipperFitter

methods.name: syndirella.slipper.SlipperFitter.SlipperFitter.fit_products
documentation: Main entry to the SlipperFitter class. This function is used to fit the products to the final library.

methods.name: syndirella.slipper.SlipperFitter.SlipperFitter.check_scaffold
documentation:
Checks if the scaffold can be minimised (no specific stereoisomer) and passes intermolecular checks.
If it cannot be minimised after 3 attempts, returns False.

methods.name: syndirella.slipper.SlipperFitter.SlipperFitter._check_intra_geom_flatness_results
documentation: This function checks the intramolecular geometry and flatness results and returns True if all are True.

methods.name: syndirella.slipper.SlipperFitter.SlipperFitter._prep_scaffold_input_df
documentation: Prepares input dataframe to Fragmenstein for the scaffold compound.

methods.name: syndirella.slipper.SlipperFitter.SlipperFitter._get_scaffold
documentation: Get scaffold compound as mol object from the input_df.

methods.name: syndirella.slipper.SlipperFitter.SlipperFitter.prep_products
documentation: This function is used to prepare the inputs for Fragmenstein.

methods.name: syndirella.slipper.SlipperFitter.SlipperFitter._prepare_input_df
documentation:
Creates the input dataframe for the placements. Will remove duplicates of names and other metadata from
synthesizer step.

methods.name: syndirella.slipper.SlipperFitter.SlipperFitter._print_diff
documentation:
This function is used to print the difference between the original number of analogues and the number of
valid analogues.

methods.name: syndirella.slipper.SlipperFitter.SlipperFitter.add_hits
documentation:
This function adds:
    hits_path as mol objects to input_df['hits']

methods.name: syndirella.slipper.SlipperFitter.SlipperFitter._place_scaffold
documentation: Places the scaffold compound, returns the mol object of the scaffold compound if successful else None.

methods.name: syndirella.slipper.SlipperFitter.SlipperFitter.place_products
documentation: This function places products using Fragmenstein.

methods.name: syndirella.slipper.SlipperFitter.SlipperFitter.check_intra_geometry
documentation:
Checks the intramolecular geometry and flatness of double bonds and aromatic rings
of each mol object using PoseBuster's implemented checks.

Checks:
Bond lengths
    The bond lengths in the input molecule are within 0.75 of the lower and 1.25 of the upper bounds determined by distance geometry
Bond angles
        The angles in the input molecule are within 0.75 of the lower and 1.25 of the upper bounds determined by distance geometry
Planar aromatic rings
    All atoms in aromatic rings with 5 or 6 members are within 0.25 Å of the closest shared plane
Planar double bonds
    The two carbons of aliphatic carbon–carbon double bonds and their four neighbours are within 0.25 Å of the closest shared plane
Internal steric clash
    The interatomic distance between pairs of non-covalently bound atoms is above 0.7 of the lower bound determined by distance geometry

methods.name: syndirella.slipper.SlipperFitter.SlipperFitter.setup_Fragmenstein
documentation: This function sets up Fragmenstein to run.

methods.name: syndirella.slipper.SlipperFitter.SlipperFitter.format_placements
documentation:
This function edits the placements by changing outcome column, adding paths to mol files, formatting scaffold
placements and checking intramolecular geometry.

methods.name: syndirella.slipper.SlipperFitter.SlipperFitter.format_scaffold_placements
documentation:
This function checks if the scaffold placement was successful. If not, attempt to replace it with the scaffold
placement performed for the scaffold check. If that placement failed, don't replace.

methods.name: syndirella.slipper.SlipperFitter.SlipperFitter.get_scaffold_check_values
documentation: This function gets the ∆∆G, comRMSD, ∆G_bound and ∆G_unbound values from the placement done by scaffold check.

methods.name: syndirella.slipper.SlipperFitter.SlipperFitter.add_paths_to_placements
documentation: Adds the absolutes paths to the .mol files.

methods.name: syndirella.slipper.SlipperFitter.SlipperFitter.merge_placements
documentation: This function merges the fragmenstein output with products csv.

methods.name: syndirella.slipper.SlipperFitter.SlipperFitter.save_placements
documentation: This function saves the placements as a .pkl.gz and .csv file.
This class is instantiated to place all the products in the template using hits_path

modules.name: syndirella.slipper.slipper_synthesizer.Labeler
documentation:
slipper_synthesizer/Labeler.py

This module contains a helper module for the SlipperSynthesizer class to label the products based on labeled atom ids
to expand or not expand.
!venv/bin/env python3

methods.name: syndirella.slipper.slipper_synthesizer.Labeler.Labeler.label_products
documentation: This is the main entry function for the Labeler class.

methods.name: syndirella.slipper.slipper_synthesizer.Labeler.Labeler.show_atoms_to_expand_and_not_expand
documentation:
This will save a png of the scaffold compound with atoms to expand colored in green and not to expand colored in
red.

methods.name: syndirella.slipper.slipper_synthesizer.Labeler.Labeler.label_products_with_atom_ids
documentation: This function will label the products with the atom ids to expand and not expand.

methods.name: syndirella.slipper.slipper_synthesizer.Labeler.Labeler._label_product_with_atom_ids
documentation: This function goes through each scaffold and checks if the compound has been expanded or not.

methods.name: syndirella.slipper.slipper_synthesizer.Labeler.Labeler._has_non_mcs_bond
documentation:
Check if the specified atom in the given molecule has a bond that is not in the MCS with a reference molecule.

```
param mol_smiles

SMILES string of the molecule to check

param ref_smiles

SMILES string of the reference molecule

param atom_id

Atom ID to check in the given molecule

return

True if the atom has a non-MCS bond, False otherwise
```

methods.name: syndirella.slipper.slipper_synthesizer.Labeler.Labeler.show_mcs_on_products
documentation: This function will output a png of the MCS on the products.

modules.name: syndirella.fairy
documentation:
syndirella.fairy.py

This module provides functions to find similar cheaper reactants, filter out
reactants based on simple filters, fingerprint generation, and others...
!/usr/bin/env python3

functions.name: syndirella.fairy.load_reactant_filters
documentation: Load necessary data

functions.name: syndirella.fairy.do_i_need_alternative_route
documentation: Check if the reaction names need an alternative route.

functions.name: syndirella.fairy.find_similar_reactants
documentation: Find additional similar reactants that are cheaper as defined in the reactant_filters.

functions.name: syndirella.fairy.get_similar_reactant
documentation: Make similar reactant to query reactant from the reactant_filters.

functions.name: syndirella.fairy.make_similar_reactant
documentation: Create a similar reactant by modifying the original reactant according to matched_details.

functions.name: syndirella.fairy.filter_molecules
documentation: Filter out reactants and return a dictionary with SMILES strings as keys and their associated scores as values.

functions.name: syndirella.fairy.format_for_output
documentation: Format the output of the filtered molecules to a dictionary with SMILES strings as keys and their scores as values.

functions.name: syndirella.fairy.simple_filters
documentation: Filter out molecules based on repeats, and non-abundant isotopes.

functions.name: syndirella.fairy.remove_hydrogen_ions
documentation: Remove molecules with only hydrogen ions.

functions.name: syndirella.fairy.remove_repeat_mols
documentation: Remove repeated molecules by checking for Tanimoto similarity of 1.

functions.name: syndirella.fairy.remove_non_abundant_isotopes
documentation: Remove molecules with non-abundant isotopes.

functions.name: syndirella.fairy.remove_chirality
documentation: Remove chirality from all molecules in the list.

functions.name: syndirella.fairy.remove_chirality_from_mol
documentation: Remove chirality from a single molecule.

functions.name: syndirella.fairy.print_diff
documentation: Print the difference between the original number of molecules and the number of valid molecules.

functions.name: syndirella.fairy.find_reaction_by_name
documentation: Find a reaction by name in the additional_rxn_options.

functions.name: syndirella.fairy.get_morgan_fingerprint
documentation: Get the fingerprint of a molecule.

functions.name: syndirella.fairy.generate_inchi_ID
documentation: This function is used to generate a unique id for the route just using the scaffold.

functions.name: syndirella.fairy.check_mol_sanity
documentation: Check if the molecule can be sanitized.

modules.name: syndirella.structure_outputs
documentation:
syndirella.structure_outputs.py

This module contains the functions used to structure the pipeline outputs.
!/usr/bin/env python3

functions.name: syndirella.structure_outputs.add_route_info
documentation: This function formats the full route into seperate columns for each reactant.

functions.name: syndirella.structure_outputs.add_outcome_info
documentation: This function adds placement information (None if not attempted).

functions.name: syndirella.structure_outputs.get_output_df
documentation:
Given the csv_path and output_dir, checks and reads in the most recent previous output df.

Format of output csv name: [name_of_input_csv]_output_YYYYMMDD_HHMM.csv

functions.name: syndirella.structure_outputs.get_scaffold_smiles
documentation: This function gets the scaffold smiles from the error, smiles, or workshop.

functions.name: syndirella.structure_outputs.get_error_info
documentation: This function gets the error type and message from the error.

functions.name: syndirella.structure_outputs.get_inchi
documentation: This function gets the inchi from the scaffold and workshop.

functions.name: syndirella.structure_outputs.check_route_to_add
documentation:
This function checks if there is a route defined in the workshop object.

Returns True if route is defined.
Returns False if route is not defined.

functions.name: syndirella.structure_outputs.check_placement_to_add
documentation:
This function checks if there is placement information in the slipper object.

Returns True if placement information exists.
Returns False if placement information does not exist.

functions.name: syndirella.structure_outputs.check_additional_info_to_add
documentation: This function checks if there is additional information in the slipper object.

functions.name: syndirella.structure_outputs.add_new_route_to_output_df
documentation:
This function adds a new row to the output dataframe. If the row contains different columns,
they are added to the output_df, and all values are filled with None if not present before.

functions.name: syndirella.structure_outputs.save_output_df
documentation:
This function saves the output dataframe to a csv file in the output directory.

Format of output csv name: [name_of_input_csv]_output_YYYYMMDD_HHMM.csv

functions.name: syndirella.structure_outputs.structure_route_outputs
documentation: This function structures the route outputs as a single row in the output dataframe.

functions.name: syndirella.structure_outputs.structure_pipeline_outputs
documentation: Structure outputs of pipeline.

modules.name: syndirella.slipper.Slipper
documentation:
syndirella.slipper.Slipper.py

This module contains the Slipper class. A slipper in this metaphor is the set of molecules that is the
scaffold of a reaction.
!venv/bin/env python3

classes.name: syndirella.slipper.Slipper.Slipper

methods.name: syndirella.slipper.Slipper.Slipper.get_products
documentation: Main entry to the Slipper class. This function is used to get the products the self.library object.

methods.name: syndirella.slipper.Slipper.Slipper.place_products
documentation: This function is used to place the products with Fragmenstein.

methods.name: syndirella.slipper.Slipper.Slipper.write_products_to_hippo
documentation:
Writes a dataframe that contains the values needed for HIPPO db input.

Returns:
    path: str : the path to the saved dataframe

methods.name: syndirella.slipper.Slipper.Slipper.check_scaffold_in_hippo
documentation: Checks if there is a scaffold in the scaffold names of the HIPPO output.

methods.name: syndirella.slipper.Slipper.Slipper._load_products_dfs
documentation: Load the products dataframes from the files in the /extra directory and putting into dict where key is step.

methods.name: syndirella.slipper.Slipper.Slipper._structure_products_for_hippo
documentation: Structures the placements or products df for HIPPO output.

methods.name: syndirella.slipper.Slipper.Slipper.find_matches
documentation: Function to find matches

methods.name: syndirella.slipper.Slipper.Slipper._put_hippo_dfs_together
documentation: Puts the HIPPO dataframes together by matching on each reaction scaffold to the correct previous step's reactant.

methods.name: syndirella.slipper.Slipper.Slipper._structure_step_for_hippo
documentation: Structures the products df for HIPPO output.

methods.name: syndirella.slipper.Slipper.Slipper.which_reactant_was_previous_product
documentation: Determine which reactant was the scaffold of the previous step.

methods.name: syndirella.slipper.Slipper.Slipper._delete_file_or_directory
documentation: Delete a file or directory at the given path.

methods.name: syndirella.slipper.Slipper.Slipper._should_delete_file
documentation: Determine if a file should be deleted based on its suffix.

methods.name: syndirella.slipper.Slipper.Slipper.clean_up_placements
documentation: This function is used to remove extra files that are generated by Fragmenstein.

methods.name: syndirella.slipper.Slipper.Slipper.get_placements_df
documentation: This function is used to get the placements dataframe which is the merged df with the products.
This class is instantiated to represent all products for a step in a route.

modules.name: syndirella.slipper.slipper_synthesizer.SlipperSynthesizer
documentation:
slipper_synthesizer/CobblersWorkshop.py

This module contains the SlipperSynthesizer class.
!venv/bin/env python3

classes.name: syndirella.slipper.slipper_synthesizer.SlipperSynthesizer.SlipperSynthesizer

methods.name: syndirella.slipper.slipper_synthesizer.SlipperSynthesizer.SlipperSynthesizer.get_products
documentation:
This function is used to find the products of the analogues of reactants. It is the main function that is
called.

methods.name: syndirella.slipper.slipper_synthesizer.SlipperSynthesizer.SlipperSynthesizer.check_product_pkl_exists
documentation: This function checks if the products pkl already exists and if so it loads it.

methods.name: syndirella.slipper.slipper_synthesizer.SlipperSynthesizer.SlipperSynthesizer.load_products
documentation: This function loads the scaffold .pkl file.

methods.name: syndirella.slipper.slipper_synthesizer.SlipperSynthesizer.SlipperSynthesizer.filter_analogues
documentation:
This function is used to go through the analogue dataframes, passing them to filter_analogues_on_smarts and
also ordering by metrics.

Finally it filters the analogues by number, making sure there aren't too many for an obscene number of products.

methods.name: syndirella.slipper.slipper_synthesizer.SlipperSynthesizer.SlipperSynthesizer.order_analogues
documentation:
This function is used to order the analogues dataframes by num atom diff to scaffold reactant of scaffold compound,
number of reactant matches found, and lead time.

methods.name: syndirella.slipper.slipper_synthesizer.SlipperSynthesizer.SlipperSynthesizer.filter_analogues_on_smarts
documentation:
This function is used to filter the analogues of reactants dataframes to make sure each analogue contains only
the SMARTS pattern of the original reactant and not the other reactant.

methods.name: syndirella.slipper.slipper_synthesizer.SlipperSynthesizer.SlipperSynthesizer.filter_analogues_by_size
documentation:
This function is used to filter the analogues dataframes by length. Need to make sure the final combination 
is less than 10,000.

If longer than 10,000, will just take the head with length of the square root of 10,000 (100).

methods.name: syndirella.slipper.slipper_synthesizer.SlipperSynthesizer.SlipperSynthesizer.cut_analogues
documentation: This function is used to cut the analogues dataframes to max_length_each by just taking the head.

methods.name: syndirella.slipper.slipper_synthesizer.SlipperSynthesizer.SlipperSynthesizer.cluster_analogues
documentation:
This function is used to cluster the analogues dataframes to max_length_each by k-means clustering.
The number of clusters is the number max length each. Might be too much...

methods.name: syndirella.slipper.slipper_synthesizer.SlipperSynthesizer.SlipperSynthesizer.combine_flags
documentation: Combine 'flag' columns

methods.name: syndirella.slipper.slipper_synthesizer.SlipperSynthesizer.SlipperSynthesizer.combine_analogues
documentation: This function is used to combine the analogues of reactants into 1 dataframe that the products are found from.

methods.name: syndirella.slipper.slipper_synthesizer.SlipperSynthesizer.SlipperSynthesizer.find_products_from_reactants
documentation: This function is used to find the products of the reactant combinations.

methods.name: syndirella.slipper.slipper_synthesizer.SlipperSynthesizer.SlipperSynthesizer.get_products_from_single_reactant
documentation: This function gets the products from a single reactant (like deprotections).

methods.name: syndirella.slipper.slipper_synthesizer.SlipperSynthesizer.SlipperSynthesizer.apply_reaction_single
documentation:
For mono-molecular reactions:
This function applies the original reaction to each row of the reactant combinations dataframe. Can return
multiple products.

methods.name: syndirella.slipper.slipper_synthesizer.SlipperSynthesizer.SlipperSynthesizer.apply_reaction
documentation:
For bimolecular reactions:
This function applies the original reaction to each row of the reactant combinations dataframe. Checks to return
only products that are sanitized.

methods.name: syndirella.slipper.slipper_synthesizer.SlipperSynthesizer.SlipperSynthesizer.calc_num_atom_diff_mcs
documentation:
This function is used to calculate the number of atoms added to scaffold
by finding the maximum common substructure (MCS) and then finding the difference in length.

methods.name: syndirella.slipper.slipper_synthesizer.SlipperSynthesizer.SlipperSynthesizer.calc_num_atom_diff_absolute
documentation: This function calculates the absolute number of atoms difference between the scaffold and scaffold.

methods.name: syndirella.slipper.slipper_synthesizer.SlipperSynthesizer.SlipperSynthesizer.filter_products
documentation:
This function is used to filter the products dataframe to remove any rows with None values. Also
removes duplicates.

methods.name: syndirella.slipper.slipper_synthesizer.SlipperSynthesizer.SlipperSynthesizer._print_diff
documentation:
This function is used to print the difference between the original number of analogues and the number of
valid analogues.

methods.name: syndirella.slipper.slipper_synthesizer.SlipperSynthesizer.SlipperSynthesizer.calculate_fingerprints
documentation: Calculate morgan fingerprints for each molecule.

methods.name: syndirella.slipper.slipper_synthesizer.SlipperSynthesizer.SlipperSynthesizer.find_similarity_groups
documentation: This is an intensive function to find all the similarity groups of the products. Could definitely be optimized.

methods.name: syndirella.slipper.slipper_synthesizer.SlipperSynthesizer.SlipperSynthesizer.assign_names_based_on_groups
documentation: Assign names to products based on their group ID, ensuring duplicates have the same name.

methods.name: syndirella.slipper.slipper_synthesizer.SlipperSynthesizer.SlipperSynthesizer.enumerate_stereoisomers
documentation: This function is used to enumerate the stereoisomers of the products.

methods.name: syndirella.slipper.slipper_synthesizer.SlipperSynthesizer.SlipperSynthesizer.save_products
documentation: This function is used to save the products dataframe as a .pkl file.

methods.name: syndirella.slipper.slipper_synthesizer.SlipperSynthesizer.SlipperSynthesizer.label_products
documentation: This function makes a new instance of the Labeler class and calls the label_products function.

This class is used to perform the whole process of finding products of the analogues of reactants.
Since the final elaborated products are 'slippers' in this analogy, the SlipperSynthesizer
is where these slippers are made.

This is supposed to be instantiated for each step in the route.

modules.name: syndirella.route.CobblersWorkshop
documentation:
syndirella.route.CobblersWorkshop.py

This module contains the CobblersWorkshop class. One instance of this object is used to describe a full route.
!/usr/bin/env python3

classes.name: syndirella.route.CobblersWorkshop.CobblersWorkshop

methods.name: syndirella.route.CobblersWorkshop.CobblersWorkshop.check_product
documentation: Checks scaffold can be converted to a molecule and can be sanitized. If not, logs an error.

methods.name: syndirella.route.CobblersWorkshop.CobblersWorkshop.check_reactants
documentation: Checks reactants can be converted to molecules and can be sanitized. If not, logs an error.

methods.name: syndirella.route.CobblersWorkshop.CobblersWorkshop.check_reaction_names
documentation: Checks reaction names are valid. If not, logs an error.

methods.name: syndirella.route.CobblersWorkshop.CobblersWorkshop.get_cobbler_bench
documentation: This function is used to get the cobbler bench for a specific step.

methods.name: syndirella.route.CobblersWorkshop.CobblersWorkshop.define_reaction
documentation: This function is used to define the reaction and finding ids and attachment atoms.

methods.name: syndirella.route.CobblersWorkshop.CobblersWorkshop.format_workshops_from_routes
documentation:
This function is used to format workshops from the combinations of reaction names and reactants.
Combinations is structured like:
[(('reaction1_name', ('reactant1_smiles','reactant2_smiles)), ('reaction2_name', ('reactant2_smiles',)), ...), # first route
(('reaction1_name', ('reactant1_smiles','reactant2_smiles)), ('reaction2_name', ('reactant2_smiles',)), ...),...]

methods.name: syndirella.route.CobblersWorkshop.CobblersWorkshop.configure_additional_routes
documentation: This function is used to configure the additional routes from the combination of all the cobbler benches.

methods.name: syndirella.route.CobblersWorkshop.CobblersWorkshop.get_additional_routes
documentation:
This function is used to get an alternative route (as a CobblersWorkshop object) if it is needed.

Args:
edit_route: bool
    If True, the reactants in the route will be directly edited via SMARTS.
    If False, an additional route containing the additional reactions specified from a Postera search will be
        returned.

methods.name: syndirella.route.CobblersWorkshop.CobblersWorkshop.log_route
documentation: This function is used to log the route.

methods.name: syndirella.route.CobblersWorkshop.CobblersWorkshop.get_final_library
documentation: This function is used to get the final library of products. It dynamically handles any number of steps.
This is the CobblersWorkshop class. It represents a full route.

modules.name: syndirella.pipeline
documentation:
syndirella.run_pipeline.py

This script contains the main pipeline for syndirella.
!/usr/bin/env python3

functions.name: syndirella.pipeline.assert_scaffold_placement
documentation: Assert that the scaffold can be placed for any stereoisomers. If not, raise an error.

functions.name: syndirella.pipeline.elaborate_from_cobbler_workshops
documentation: Does elaboration once the cobbler workshops are created.

functions.name: syndirella.pipeline.start_elaboration
documentation: Starts the elaboration of a single compound by checking if it can be placed.

functions.name: syndirella.pipeline.elaborate_compound_with_manual_routes
documentation: This function is used to elaborate a single compound using a manually defined route.

functions.name: syndirella.pipeline.elaborate_compound_full_auto
documentation: This function is used to elaborate a single compound.

functions.name: syndirella.pipeline.run_pipeline
documentation:
Run the whole syndirella pipeline! 👑

Settings dict should have these keys:
  csv_path=settings['input'],
  output_dir=settings['output'],
  template_dir=settings['templates'],
  hits_path=settings['hits_path'],
  metadata_path=settings['metadata'],
  batch_num=settings['batch_num'],
  additional_columns=['compound_set'], # Will always be compound_set
  manual_routes=settings['manual'],
  atom_diff_min=settings['atom_diff_min'],
  atom_diff_max=settings['atom_diff_max']

modules.name: syndirella.route.Library
documentation:
syndirella.route.Library.py

This module contains the Library class. This class contains information about the analogue library. It will create the
analogue library from the Reaction object. It will also store the analogue library as a .pkl file.
!venv/bin/env python3

classes.name: syndirella.route.Library.Library

methods.name: syndirella.route.Library.Library.create_library
documentation: This function is used to create the analogue library from the Reaction object.

methods.name: syndirella.route.Library.Library.process_analogues
documentation:
This function puts list of analogues in dataframe and does SMART checking to check if the analogues contains
the SMARTS pattern of the original reactant and against all other reactants SMARTS.

methods.name: syndirella.route.Library.Library.check_analogue_contains_smarts_pattern
documentation:
This function is used to check if the analogues contains the original reactant. Will return
dictionary of boolean values and the number of matches.

methods.name: syndirella.route.Library.Library.filter_analogues
documentation: This function is used to filter out analogues.

methods.name: syndirella.route.Library.Library.print_diff
documentation:
This function is used to print the difference between the original number of analogues and the number of
valid analogues.

methods.name: syndirella.route.Library.Library.filter_on_substructure_filters
documentation: This function is used to filter out analogues that do not pass the substructure filters.

methods.name: syndirella.route.Library.Library.check_analogue_contains_other_reactant_smarts_pattern
documentation: This function is used to check if the analogues contains the SMARTS patterns of the other reactant.

methods.name: syndirella.route.Library.Library.check_analogue_contains_all_smarts_patterns
documentation: This function is used to check if the analogues contains all the SMARTS patterns of the other reactants.

methods.name: syndirella.route.Library.Library.check_analogues_pkl_exists
documentation: This function is used to check if the analogue library has already been created and saved as a .pkl file.

methods.name: syndirella.route.Library.Library._find_analogues_pkl_name
documentation:
Checks if the analogue library was already created and saved as a .pkl.gz file. Returns the path to the
.pkl.gz file.

methods.name: syndirella.route.Library.Library._find_products_pkl_name
documentation:
This function is used to find the name of the products .pkl file by comparing the reactant to the scaffold with
bit vector similarity.

methods.name: syndirella.route.Library.Library.save_library
documentation: This function is used to save the analogue library as a .pkl file in self.extra_dir_path

methods.name: syndirella.route.Library.Library.load_library
documentation:
This function is used to load the analogue library from a .pkl file. Returns a dictionary of the smiles as keys
and the lead time (if found) as float. Otherwise return lead time as None.

methods.name: syndirella.route.Library.Library.save
documentation: This function saves the library object in the self.extra_output_dir_path.

methods.name: syndirella.route.Library.Library.load
documentation: This function loads the library object. Will have to load all library objects

This class contains information about the analogue library. It will create the analogue library from the Reaction
object. It will also store the analogue library as a .pkl file.

modules.name: syndirella.route.CobblerBench
documentation:
syndirella.CobblerBench.py

This module contains the CobblerBench class. One instance is made for each step.
!venv/bin/env python3

classes.name: syndirella.route.CobblerBench.CobblerBench

methods.name: syndirella.route.CobblerBench.CobblerBench._make_reactant_mols
documentation: This function is used to make reactant molecules from the input SMILES.

methods.name: syndirella.route.CobblerBench.CobblerBench.check_reaction
documentation: This function is used to check the reaction is valid.

methods.name: syndirella.route.CobblerBench.CobblerBench.define_reaction
documentation: This function is used to define the reaction and finding ids and attachment atoms.

methods.name: syndirella.route.CobblerBench.CobblerBench.get_additional_reactions
documentation:
Gets the additional reaction by outputting edited reactants via SMARTS and new reaction names all controlled
by the Reaction class.

methods.name: syndirella.route.CobblerBench.CobblerBench.find_analogues
documentation: This function is used to find analogues of any step along the route.

This class is used to perform the whole process of finding analogues of reactants. Since the final elaborated products
are 'slippers' in this analogy, the CobblerBench is where these slippers are made.

It is given a step of a full route.

modules.name: syndirella.route.Reaction
documentation:
syndirella.route.Reaction.py

This module contains the Reaction class. One instance of this object is used to describe a single reaction.
!/usr/bin/env python3

classes.name: syndirella.route.Reaction.Reaction

methods.name: syndirella.route.Reaction.Reaction.alt_reaction
documentation: This function is used to determine if an additional reaction is specified.

methods.name: syndirella.route.Reaction.Reaction.get_additional_reactions
documentation: This function edits the reactants to make an additional reaction.

methods.name: syndirella.route.Reaction.Reaction.check_reaction_can_produce_product
documentation: This function is used to check if the new reactants can simply produce a scaffold from the labeled reaction.

methods.name: syndirella.route.Reaction.Reaction.edit_reactant
documentation: Directly edits the reactant to replace SMARTS matched atoms to new SMARTS.

methods.name: syndirella.route.Reaction.Reaction.find_attachment_id_for_reactant
documentation:
This function is used to find the attachment indices of a single reactant in the reaction.
\:param reactant: a single reactant molecule
\:returns a list of tuples (attachmentIdx_whole, attachmentIdx_subMol)

methods.name: syndirella.route.Reaction.Reaction.find_attachment_ids_for_all_reactants
documentation:
This function is used to find the attachment indices of all reactants in the reaction.
:returns a list of lists, each containing tuples of attachment indices for each reactant

methods.name: syndirella.route.Reaction.Reaction.format_matched_smarts_to_index
documentation: Formats matched smarts to reactant by using reactant index in smarts as the key.

methods.name: syndirella.route.Reaction.Reaction.find_reaction_atoms_for_all_reactants
documentation:
This function is used to find the reaction atoms of both reactants. And how those atoms correspond to the SMARTS
pattern associated with the reaction.

This class contains information about the reaction. It is used to find the atoms on the reactants involved in
the reaction, check the reaction atoms are the ones that are supposed to be connected.

modules.name: syndirella.Cobbler
documentation:
syndirella.cobbler.Cobbler.py

This module contains the Cobbler class. It is used to perform retrosynthetic analysis of the scaffold compound. It handles
Postera searches, including multiple elaboration searches.
!/usr/bin/env python3

classes.name: syndirella.Cobbler.Cobbler

methods.name: syndirella.Cobbler.Cobbler.get_routes
documentation: This function is used to get the routes for the scaffold compound. Main function that is called.

methods.name: syndirella.Cobbler.Cobbler.get_route
documentation: From Postera routes, choose the route, then create the cobblers workshop.

methods.name: syndirella.Cobbler.Cobbler.create_cobblers_workshop_from_Postera
documentation: Creates a cobblers workshop from a route.

methods.name: syndirella.Cobbler.Cobbler.get_passing_routes
documentation: Gets the passing routes from the routes based on their reaction names are in the allowed list.

methods.name: syndirella.Cobbler.Cobbler.filter_routes
documentation:
Filters routes that contain reactants with a single atom, or an intramolecular reaction that is coded as two
reactants.

methods.name: syndirella.Cobbler.Cobbler.choose_route
documentation: Gets first route from List if all the reaction names are allowed and if doesn't contain a single atom as reactant.

methods.name: syndirella.Cobbler.Cobbler._print_route
documentation: This function is used to print the route.

methods.name: syndirella.Cobbler.Cobbler.save
documentation: Pickle cobbler object, so it can be read later.
A single cobbler represents 1 scaffold compound design. It can contain multiple routes (CobblerWorkshop objects).

This module contains the functionality for a database search. Can do it via Postera or another database (smallworld, etc.).

methods.name: syndirella.DatabaseSearch.DatabaseSearch.get_resp_json

modules.name: syndirella.SMARTSHandler
documentation:
SMARTSHandler.py

This module contains the SMARTSHandler class. This class contains information about the reaction SMARTS.
!/usr/bin/env python3

methods.name: syndirella.SMARTSHandler.SMARTSHandler.assign_reactants_w_rxn_smarts

returns.name:
dict[
                                                             str, tuple[Mol, list[int], str]] | None | dict[
                                                             Any | None, None]

documentation:
This function is used to assign the reactant number to input reactants using the reaction SMARTS. For now it
only supports bimolecular reactions.

methods.name: syndirella.SMARTSHandler.SMARTSHandler.seperate_matching_reactants
documentation:
This function is used to fix edge cases:
- to separate reactants that match both reactant SMARTS.
- one reactant matches both reactant SMARTS.

These found_1 and found_2 dictionaries are a bit confusing:
Key: 'r1' or 'r2'
Value: False if no match, List of atom indices within reactant that match the SMARTS of that reactant.

methods.name: syndirella.SMARTSHandler.SMARTSHandler.check_found_reactants
documentation:
This function checks that both reactants have been found. It raises a warning if both reactants are matched
to the same reactant SMARTS.

Returns False when both reactants match both reactant SMARTS.

methods.name: syndirella.SMARTSHandler.SMARTSHandler.find_matching_atoms
documentation: This function finds the matched atoms in a reactant against both reactant SMARTS.

methods.name: syndirella.SMARTSHandler.SMARTSHandler.format_matched_reactant_for_one
documentation: Formats matched reactants for one reactant.

Postera.py

This module contains the functionality for a Postera search.

classes.name: syndirella.Postera.Postera

methods.name: syndirella.Postera.Postera.perform_route_search
documentation: This function is used to perform the route query.

methods.name: syndirella.Postera.Postera.perform_database_search
documentation: This function is used to perform the Postera search using the database_search_function.

methods.name: syndirella.Postera.Postera.perform_superstructure_search
documentation: This function is used to perform the Postera superstructure search.

methods.name: syndirella.Postera.Postera.perform_exact_search
documentation: This function is used to perform the Postera exact search.

methods.name: syndirella.Postera.Postera.perform_exact_batch_search
documentation: Performs exact search with a batch of smiles.

methods.name: syndirella.Postera.Postera.structure_output
documentation: Formats output where key is the smiles and value is the lead time. Could add other purchase info if interested.

methods.name: syndirella.Postera.Postera.get_resp_json
documentation: Directly get the response json from a request, with retry mechanism for handling 429 status code.

methods.name: syndirella.Postera.Postera.get_search_results
documentation:
Recursively get all pages for the endpoint until reach null next page or
the max_pages threshold.

methods.name: syndirella.Postera.Postera.make_query
documentation: This function is used to make a query to the Postera database.

methods.name: syndirella.Postera.Postera.save_database_search
documentation: This function is used to save the results of the Postera search as a .csv file.

methods.name: syndirella.Postera.Postera.load_database_search
documentation: This function is used to load the results of the Postera search from a .csv file.

This class contains information about the Postera search. It will perform the Postera search using the
perform_database_search function. It will also store the results of the Postera search as a .csv file.

modules.name: syndirella.check_inputs
documentation:
syndirella.check_inputs.py

This module contains the functions used to check the inputs for running the pipeline.
!/usr/bin/env python3

functions.name: syndirella.check_inputs.check_csv
documentation: Make sure that the csv path exists, can be read, and contains the required columns.

functions.name: syndirella.check_inputs.metadata_dict
documentation: Get the metadata dictionary from the metadata file, checking that it contains the required columns.

functions.name: syndirella.check_inputs.check_template_paths
documentation: Get the exact template paths, checking that they exist in the template directory.

functions.name: syndirella.check_inputs.fill_in_product
documentation: Fill in the scaffold for the given step.

functions.name: syndirella.check_inputs.check_route
documentation: Checks that the route is in the correct format and fills in missing products if needed.

functions.name: syndirella.check_inputs.check_manual
documentation: Check that the manual dataframe is in the correct format, otherwise raise errors.

functions.name: syndirella.check_inputs.check_hit_names
documentation: Check that the hit names are found within SDF.

functions.name: syndirella.check_inputs.check_apo_template
documentation: Check that the template is actually apo (containing no LIG).

functions.name: syndirella.check_inputs.format_additional_info
documentation: This function is used to format the additional info from the dataframe into a dictionary.
# Additional functions for pipeline

functions.name: syndirella.check_inputs.get_exact_hit_names
documentation: Get the exact hit name to use for placement.

functions.name: syndirella.check_inputs.get_template_path
documentation: Get the exact template path to use for placement.

functions.name: syndirella.check_inputs.check_additional_columns
documentation: Check that the additional columns are in the csv.

functions.name: syndirella.check_inputs.format_manual_route
documentation: Format route to output reactants, reaction names, and products.

functions.name: syndirella.check_inputs.check_pipeline_inputs
documentation: Check the inputs for the pipeline.
